title: VB base tokens: borders + scrollbars + cursors (Decorated Layers #2)
description: Base tier in vanilla-breeze: CSS-first token categories for animated borders (spin/pulse/march/hue-cycle/breathe/trace via data-border attribute), scrollbars (scrollbar-color/width), cursors (custom + opt-in JS spotlight). Ships the COMPLETE border token vocabulary incl. extreme-only names. Reduced-motion contract. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-vb-base-tokens-borders-scrollbars-cursors-design.md. Target repo: vanilla-breeze.
acceptance_criteria: data-border base values render via VB CSS; scrollbar+cursor tokens themeable on :root; reduced-motion zeroes animations; specimen demo (port of borders-cursors-scrollbars.html); complete token set shipped.
notes: Implementation complete; PR open: https://github.com/ProfPowell/vanilla-breeze/pull/1 (vanilla-breeze, base work/2026-05-21). Awaiting merge.

title: Decorated Layers — base-in-VB + extreme packages across visual surfaces
description: Umbrella epic. Family of platform-first, theme-aware decoration systems across surfaces (effect/background/border/scrollbar/cursor) in two strata: base (vanilla-breeze tokens/CSS) + extreme (opt-in packages). VB ships the complete token vocabulary and the base; packages embed the base for standalone. Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-decorated-layers-design.md. Settled decisions: data-* attributes (data-border/data-background) as author hooks; data-background canonical (data-bg alias); background gets a light CSS base in VB; geometry authored in VB + embedded; spotlight in VB base; 'Decorated Layers' is internal-only. Sub-project #1 (umbrella) + specs #2/#4 are written.

title: Shared perimeter/shape geometry primitive (Decorated Layers #3)
description: perimeterPath()/perimeterSampler() for an element's outer shape (rounded rects MVP; shape()/path() later). Authored once in vanilla-breeze ('the shape'), embedded into bg-wc/border-wc for standalone. border-wc strokes it; bg-wc masks shaders to it. Spec ref: umbrella §4 + border-wc spec §4. Target: vanilla-breeze (+embedded).
notes: Implementation complete; PR open: https://github.com/ProfPowell/vanilla-breeze/pull/2 (vanilla-breeze, base work/2026-05-21). 16 unit tests; source-only. Awaiting merge.

title: border-wc component — extreme border effects (Decorated Layers #4)
description: New sibling component @profpowell/border-wc (new repo ~/src/border-wc). Extreme tier only: squiggle (SVG), draw (SVG), sparks (Canvas), liquid (WebGL, deferred). CSS effects (gradient/glow/dash) delegate to VB base (#2) as spin/pulse/march — not reimplemented. Consumes the shared perimeter primitive (#3); embeds VB base for standalone. oklch-safe color parsing (pixel readback). Spec: docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-border-wc-component-design.md.
acceptance_criteria: border-wc.js dispatcher + squiggle/draw/sparks working; reduced-motion guard; IntersectionObserver pause for sparks; sibling-standard toolchain; published as @profpowell/border-wc.
notes:
Implementation complete + verified in local repo ~/src/border-wc (branch main, ~12 commits): element+dispatcher, draw/squiggle/sparks effects, embedded perimeter, oklch-safe canvas color, reduced-motion, 8 Playwright specs pass, lint clean, cem manifest, demo renders all 3 effects. No GitHub remote yet — awaiting publish decision.
Published: https://github.com/ProfPowell/border-wc (public). CI green (lint/audit/test/build); Pages live + verified: https://profpowell.github.io/border-wc/ (all 3 effects render, no errors). npm publish is a later step.

title: Make demo navigation consistent across gallery, docs, and demos
description:
User reports that clicking through to the demo pages is 'not consistent.' Investigate and unify navigation:
- The gallery (docs/index.html) and api.html link to '../demos/' (the demos index). Confirm demos/index.html links back to the gallery/docs consistently and that all 32 demo pages have a consistent way back (currently bespoke per-aesthetic, many may have no nav at all).
- Decide on a consistent pattern: a minimal, unobtrusive 'back to gallery' affordance on each demo that doesn't break the bespoke aesthetic, plus a consistent demos/index.html that links to gallery + docs.
- Verify all cross-links resolve (no 404s) from demos/ -> ../docs/ etc.
acceptance_criteria: Navigation between gallery, docs, and demos is consistent and discoverable; every demo has a consistent way back to the gallery; demos/index.html links to gallery + docs; no broken cross-links.

title: Docs hero: default dark + make the data-bg animation visible around the glass panel
description:
The hero text now sits in a themed glass panel for contrast (done), but two issues remain on the API docs hero (docs/api.html):

1. The API page defaults to light (theme-picker auto) while the gallery (docs/index.html) defaults to dark via preferDarkByDefault() in docs/gallery.js. Give api.html the same first-load dark nudge for consistency (it shares docs-entry.js, which has no such logic).
2. With the centered glass panel, the data-bg='mesh-gradient' animation is barely visible (washed out, especially in light). Rework so the animation actually showcases the component — e.g. offset/left-align the panel like the gallery hero so the animation reads alongside, and/or bump data-bg-intensity. Keep the themed-scrim contrast guarantee.
acceptance_criteria: API docs hero defaults to dark on first load (matching the gallery); the data-bg animation is clearly visible around/alongside the text panel in both light and dark; text contrast remains guaranteed in all themes.

title: Sync embedded perimeter.js copies (bg-wc, border-wc) with VB Phase 2
description: vanilla-breeze src/lib/perimeter.js gained the Phase 2 segment model + shape builders (ak6.6). bg-wc and border-wc embed a copy that still has only the uniform rounded-rect API. Sync the embedded copies when those consumers need non-uniform/clip-path shapes. Additive (existing roundedRect* signatures unchanged), so not urgent.

title: npm deprecate @profpowell/gl-wc -> bg-wc
description: After publishing @profpowell/bg-wc@0.2.0, run: npm deprecate @profpowell/gl-wc "renamed to @profpowell/bg-wc". Manual step; needs npm auth. Follow-up from ak6.5.

title: perimeter: advanced shapes — asymmetric/elliptical radii, clip-path, non-rect (Decorated Layers, perimeter Phase 2)
description: High-value follow-up to ak6.2 (src/lib/perimeter.js). After the uniform-rounded-rect MVP lands, extend the perimeter primitive to the extreme shapes that show off border-wc/bg-wc: (1) asymmetric per-corner border-radius (8-value parse); (2) elliptical radii rx!=ry (arc-length over ellipse segments); (3) clip-path: shape()/path() tracing (read getComputedStyle(host).clipPath); (4) arbitrary polygons / clip-path: polygon(). The MVP API (perimeterPath/perimeterSampler + the t-contract) is designed to extend to these without breaking consumers. Spec §5 of docs/superpowers/specs/2026-05-25-perimeter-geometry-ak6.2-design.md. Target: vanilla-breeze src/lib/perimeter.js.
close_reason: perimeter Phase 2 shipped in vanilla-breeze (PR #3 merged to work/2026-05-21): segment-model core + asymmetric/elliptical radii, clip-path polygon/circle/ellipse/inset/path()/shape(), shape-detecting DOM wrappers. 40/40 tests; uniform path reimplemented on core with byte-identical output.

title: gl-wc -> bg-wc rename + data-background alignment (Decorated Layers #6)
description: Breaking rename: @profpowell/gl-wc -> @profpowell/bg-wc; <gl-wc> -> <bg-wc>; --gl-wc-* -> --bg-wc-*; data-bg -> data-background (canonical) with data-bg/--gl-wc-* kept as deprecated aliases. Ship final gl-wc that subclass-aliases bg-wc with a deprecation warning. Sequenced late to avoid churn during border work. Spec ref: umbrella §7.
acceptance_criteria: @profpowell/bg-wc published; <gl-wc> aliases <bg-wc> with deprecation warning; data-bg + --gl-wc-* still work; docs/site updated to bg-wc.
close_reason: gl-wc->bg-wc rename shipped (PR #19 merged): <bg-wc> canonical, <gl-wc>/data-bg/--gl-wc-*/gl-wc:* deprecated aliases warn-once. @profpowell/bg-wc@0.2.0. GitHub repo renamed to bg-wc, Pages live at /bg-wc. 12/12 tests.

title: data-border binder — attribute layer for border-wc (Decorated Layers #5)
description: Mirror src/data-bg.js: scan + MutationObserver for data-border; base values are pure VB CSS (no injection), extreme values inject <border-wc effect=...>. Routes base->CSS, extreme->component. Spec ref: border-wc spec §6. Target: border-wc/gl-wc.
close_reason: data-border-effect binder shipped in border-wc (PR #1 merged): opt-in ./attr entry applies extreme effects to any annotated element; base values ignored. 15/15 tests, CI green.
